# UC-13.5.12 · Splunk Observability Cloud Detector Health

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep a master list of the automated alarms that watch your cloud software, and we raise a clear flag when those alarms go quiet, scream too much, or drift from the teams and services they are supposed to protect.*

---

## Description

Splunk Observability Cloud detectors are the programmable alerting backbone for application observability, yet they degrade in ways dashboards rarely surface: silent long-running mutes, stale conditions that still reference renamed metrics after refactors, alert fatigue when noisy detectors fire hundreds of critical incidents weekly, abandoned programs left behind by departed engineers, mismatched detector severity versus real service criticality, and coverage gaps where production tags never match detector filters. This use case is the governance layer that inventories detectors, correlates incidents and audits, and highlights when the very alerts meant to protect revenue paths stop telling the truth. It complements UC-13.5.1 trace-duration anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 trace error-rate monitoring, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness checks, UC-13.5.4 RED metrics analytics, UC-13.5.5 log-trace correlation, UC-13.5.6 fan-out and depth, UC-13.5.7 service-map RED views, UC-13.5.8 APM database query latency, UC-13.5.9 real-user Core Web Vitals, UC-13.5.10 browser JavaScript error clustering, UC-13.5.11 synthetic transaction SLA enforcement, and UC-13.5.16 multi-window SLO burn-rate alerting by focusing on detector inventory hygiene and meta-alerting, the alerts that watch whether your detectors remain faithful.

## Value

Executive and platform stakeholders receive a tamper-resistant ledger proving which Observability Cloud detectors still align with production criticality, which teams own remediation, and where mute or staleness drift could violate internal alerting policies. Site reliability engineers regain signal because noisy detectors are quantified before they crowd out real incidents, while quiet tier-one gaps are caught before an outage proves the program was hollow. Risk and audit partners see CSV-backed ownership, infrastructure-as-code flags, and correlated Splunk Alerts datamodel activity, which closes the loop between Observability incidents and enterprise alerting narratives without pretending a single UI screen replaces governance.

## Implementation

Land signalfx:detectors snapshots via Detector REST pagination with vault-managed OAuth, mirror signalfx:incidents and signalfx:audit_log, forward Terraform Cloud audit to HTTP Event Collector, accelerate Splunk CIM Alerts for the tstats arm, maintain splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv with criticality and infrastructure-as-code columns, save observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_inventory_health hourly, alert on CRITICAL and HIGH health_class after tuning, and archive weekly governance exports.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_inventory_health with hourly schedule, governance dashboard on observability index mirrors, weekly CSV export of CRITICAL and HIGH rows to restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab Observability org, create a tier-one detector row in splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv without infrastructure-as-code relief, stop generating matching incidents for ninety-five days while snapshots still ingest, and confirm observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_inventory_health returns CRITICAL with non-null recommended_action within two hourly runs.

### Negative scenario

After restoring realistic incident cadence for the same detector or marking is_iac_managed true with matching Terraform audit evidence, confirm health_class returns to LOW for well-behaved services and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to CRITICAL and HIGH only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineering, site reliability leaders who set paging policy, identity administrators who rotate OAuth clients for Observability Cloud, and the teams that operate Splunk Enterprise search heads hosting governance searches. This use case governs the detector inventory itself inside Splunk Observability Cloud: the programmable alert objects whose SignalFlow programs evaluate streams, open incidents, route to teams, and silently fail when muted, mis-scoped, renamed, or abandoned. It is deliberately not UC-13.5.1 trace-duration anomaly detection, not UC-13.5.2 trace error-rate narratives, not UC-13.5.3 trace completeness integrity, not UC-13.5.4 RED saturation analytics, not UC-13.5.5 log-trace correlation, not UC-13.5.6 fan-out depth, not UC-13.5.7 service-map RED overlays, not UC-13.5.8 APM database query latency, not UC-13.5.9 real-user Core Web Vitals, not UC-13.5.10 browser JavaScript error clustering, not UC-13.5.11 synthetic multi-step SLA journeys, and not UC-13.5.16 multi-window SLO burn-rate engines. Those siblings watch application behavior; this control watches whether the alerting backbone stays trustworthy.

Scope boundaries require honest data contracts. Land Detector REST exports as structured events with sourcetype signalfx:detectors on a schedule tight enough to catch configuration drift but gentle enough to respect API quotas. Mirror Observability incident history as signalfx:incidents so Splunk can measure firing cadence and recency. Mirror realm audit narratives as signalfx:audit_log for create, update, and mute verbs. Forward Terraform Cloud organization audit streams that mention signalfx_detector resources so infrastructure-as-code ownership is visible beside UI edits. Supplement org-level Observability usage telemetry such as sf_metricsTransmittedDaily, sf_dpmTodayLastModified, and sf_streamLagAlerts when your pipeline exposes OrgMetrics, because sudden ingest or lag shifts often precede silent detector starvation even when individual programs look syntactically valid.

RBAC and secrets: scripted inputs that call GET /v2/detector with pagination must use a dedicated OAuth client stored in a vault, rotated on a documented cadence, and never embedded in repository plaintext. Service accounts may appear as creators; carry human routing in splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv instead of pretending the API creator field is always a person. Legal review should confirm detector names, team identifiers, and customer tags are acceptable in Splunk retention windows.

Index design: consolidate governed feeds into index=observability with sourcetypes signalfx:detectors, signalfx:incidents, signalfx:audit_log, signalfx:teams when you export roster metadata, terraform_cloud:audit or tfc:audit for infrastructure-as-code evidence, and optional prometheus or internal metrics sourcetypes for OrgMetrics. Retain at least thirty days hot for mute and firing history correlation, ninety days for governance attestations, and align cold storage with observability policy.

Lookup hygiene: maintain splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv with columns detector_id, owner_team, service_criticality, is_iac_managed, scheduled_quiet_window, and expected_trigger_freq_per_30d. Refresh the file through lookup editor change control or automated git sync. Document how scheduled_quiet_window encodes approved maintenance vocabulary so the search can classify intentional quiet periods without muting real abandonment.

Risk briefing: a detector that never fires may be a blessing or a broken condition; this search treats long absence of incidents on tier-one services as governance risk unless infrastructure-as-code flags explain the quiet state. Conversely, a detector that fires hundreds of critical incidents weekly is not merely noisy; it erodes on-call attention for every other control. Mute drift is a compliance concern because auditors ask whether alerting coverage matches production reality.

Collector posture: implement scripted input pagination against GET /v2/detector?limit=1000 following next links until exhaustion, stamp export_batch_id on each snapshot, and log HTTP status families without printing bearer tokens. Pair Splunk_TA_signalfx or equivalent forwarders for audit feeds if your enterprise standardizes on that path. For Terraform Cloud, send organization audit JSON to HTTP Event Collector with a sourcetype that preserves resource addresses.

Differentiation recap: detector inventory governance and meta-alerting on the health of Observability Cloud detectors themselves, not primary APM trace analytics, not RUM vitals, not synthetic journeys, not SLO burn math in isolation.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch lanes Splunk can fuse each run: periodic detector inventory snapshots, incident history for firing cadence, realm audit rows for mute and configuration mutations, accelerated Splunk Common Information Model Alerts summaries for cross-platform correlation of signatures that mirror detector names, and Terraform Cloud audit exports for infrastructure-as-code provenance.

Detector inventory lane: schedule a Python or shell scripted input every fifteen to sixty minutes depending on tenant size. Call Observability Cloud Detector REST under /v2/detector with limit and offset or cursor pagination until all pages return. Normalize id, name, creator, team routing, program text checksum, disabled flags, and mute metadata into signalfx:detectors events. Rotate OAuth access tokens through your vault and fail closed when refresh fails rather than replaying stale tokens that return 401 storms.

Incident lane: enable incident export or mirror Observability incident webhooks into signalfx:incidents with detector identifiers, severity, lifecycle timestamps, and acknowledgement hints. Preserve enough fields to compute seven-day critical counts and last-fire epochs per detector.

Audit lane: configure realm audit log export through Splunk_TA_signalfx or direct HTTPS pull jobs landing signalfx:audit_log. Retain actor, verb, target identifiers, and human-readable messages for mute operations. If retention in Observability is shorter than thirty days, mirror early into Splunk so this governance search still sees a full month.

Alerts data model lane: accelerate Alerts in Splunk Enterprise when Enterprise Security or a compatible mapping populates signatures that correspond to Observability incidents. The tstats arm counts recent alert volume keyed by Alerts.signature to corroborate noisy detectors even when incident export hiccups.

Terraform lane: stream Terraform Cloud organization audit logs to HTTP Event Collector with sourcetype terraform_cloud:audit. Filter for workspaces that manage signalfx_detector resources. Include run metadata and user actors so infrastructure-as-code ownership joins cleanly to detector_id when your inventory lookup aligns resource addresses.

OrgMetrics lane: optional sidecar jobs can land sf_metricsTransmittedDaily, sf_dpmTodayLastModified, and sf_streamLagAlerts into the same index with distinct sourcetypes for executive dashboards; wire those panels outside this search if cardinality is high, but reference them in runbooks when stream lag precedes incident gaps.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE generously for SignalFlow program text if you choose to index it, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS aligned to API millisecond epochs, and FIELDALIAS maps for flattened versus dotted JSON.

Pre-save validation: index=observability sourcetype=signalfx:detectors earliest=-1h must return one batch per interval; signalfx:incidents must show recent production fires; signalfx:audit_log must show a known test mute within minutes; inputlookup splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv must list tier-one services you care about.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_inventory_health with an hourly schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-30d@d, dispatch latest=now, and alert when health_class is CRITICAL or HIGH after calibration in Step 4. Throttle repeated detector_id rows for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action and lanes_seen in pager narratives so responders open detector programs without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans detector snapshots, incident history, audit mute rows, Alerts datamodel counts, and Terraform audit exports so a silent single lane does not hide governance drift. coalesce ladders unify detector_id, detector_name, creator_id, team, and service across exporter variants. sort plus streamstats applies a trailing thirty-day incident pulse sum per detector. stats collapses to one row per detector with last incident time, last metadata refresh, seven-day critical sums, and lane coverage. join enriches service_criticality, infrastructure-as-code flags, and scheduled quiet hints from splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv. eventstats computes the org-wide median of rolling thirty-day triggers for context. case maps tier-one stale detectors without infrastructure-as-code relief to CRITICAL, excessive seven-day critical firings to HIGH, long mute or stale update patterns to MEDIUM, and healthy or calendar-approved quiet to LOW.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.12 Observability Cloud detector governance: inventory snapshots, incident history, mute audit, Alerts datamodel facet, Terraform change audit. Tunables stale_trigger_days=90 mute_stale_days=7 earliest=-30d@d")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:detectors" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(detectorId, id, detector_id, resourceId, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(name, detectorName, label, detector_name, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(creatorId, createdBy, author, creator_id, "")))
      | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(teamId, team, notification_team, "")))
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service, service_name, sf_service, "")))
      | eval is_muted_now=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(isMuted, muted, is_muted, "false"))),"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval upd_epoch=if(isnotnull(lastUpdated), tonumber(lastUpdated,10), if(isnotnull('lastUpdated'), tonumber('lastUpdated',10), _time))
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="det_inv"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:incidents" earliest=-30d@d latest=now
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(detectorId, detector_id, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(detectorName, detector_name, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(incidentCreator, creator_id, "")))
      | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(responderTeam, teamId, team, "")))
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceId, service, "")))
      | eval sev_norm=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, level, ""))))
      | eval is_crit_fire=if(_time>=relative_time(now(),"-7d@d") AND match(sev_norm,"critical|severe|error"),1,0)
      | eval is_muted_now=0
      | eval upd_epoch=_time
      | eval inc_pulse=1
      | eval lane="inc_hist"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:audit_log" earliest=-30d@d latest=now (action="detectorUpdated" OR action="detectorCreated" OR action="detectorMuted" OR match(lower(message),"detector|mute"))
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(targetId, objectId, detectorId, resourceId, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(targetName, detectorName, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(actorId, userEmail, "")))
      | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(teamScope, "")))
      | eval service=""
      | eval is_muted_now=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(action,message,""))),"mute|disable|silence"),1,0)
      | eval upd_epoch=_time
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="aud_mute"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count AS alert_dm_hit latest(_time) AS dm_last FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Alerts.signature
      | eval detector_id=md5(trim(toString(Alerts.signature)))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(Alerts.signature))
      | eval creator_id="splunk_cim_alerts"
      | eval team="es_correlation"
      | eval service="mapped_signature"
      | eval is_muted_now=0
      | eval upd_epoch=dm_last
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="alerts_dm"
      | eval _time=dm_last
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire alert_dm_hit ]
    [ search index=observability (sourcetype="terraform_cloud:audit" OR sourcetype="tfc:audit") earliest=-30d@d latest=now (resource_type="signalfx_detector" OR match(_raw,"signalfx_detector|detector"))
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(resource_id, resource_name, detectorId, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(resource_name, detector_name, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(user_name, actor, "")))
      | eval team="terraform_cloud"
      | eval service="iac_pipelined"
      | eval is_muted_now=0
      | eval upd_epoch=_time
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="tf_audit"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
| eval detector_id=if(len(detector_id)<2 AND len(detector_name)>0, md5(detector_name), detector_id)
| eval detector_name=coalesce(nullif(detector_name,""), "unnamed_detector")
| eval creator_id=coalesce(nullif(creator_id,""), "unknown_creator")
| eval team=coalesce(nullif(team,""), "unassigned_team")
| eval service=coalesce(nullif(service,""), "unscoped_service")
| eval is_crit_fire=coalesce(is_crit_fire,0)
| sort 0 + detector_id + _time
| streamstats time_window=2592000 global=f sum(inc_pulse) AS triggers_30d BY detector_id
| stats
    max(eval(if(lane=="inc_hist",_time,null()))) AS last_incident_ts
    max(eval(if(lane=="det_inv",_time,null()))) AS last_det_refresh
    max(upd_epoch) AS last_metadata_epoch
    max(is_muted_now) AS snapshot_muted
    sum(is_crit_fire) AS critical_fires_7d
    sum(inc_pulse) AS incident_events_30d
    max(triggers_30d) AS trigger_roll30
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
    max(alert_dm_hit) AS alerts_dm_hits
  BY detector_id
| eval days_since_update=round((now()-last_metadata_epoch)/86400,2)
| join type=left max=0 detector_id
    [| inputlookup splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv
     | fields detector_id owner_team service_criticality is_iac_managed scheduled_quiet_window expected_trigger_freq_per_30d ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, team)
| eval service_criticality=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_criticality, "tier3"))))
| eval iac_flag=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(is_iac_managed, "false"))),"true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval quiet_calendar=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(scheduled_quiet_window, ""))))
| eval in_quiet_window=if(match(quiet_calendar,"maint|blackout|holiday|training|sandbox"),1,0)
| eval days_since_trigger=if(isnull(last_incident_ts), 999, round((now()-last_incident_ts)/86400,2))
| eval stale_no_fire=if(isnull(last_incident_ts) OR days_since_trigger>90,1,0)
| eval mute_long=if(snapshot_muted==1 AND days_since_update>7,1,0)
| eval trig_metric=trigger_roll30
| eventstats median(trig_metric) AS org_median_trig30
| eval health_class=case(
    service_criticality=="tier1" AND stale_no_fire==1 AND iac_flag==0, "CRITICAL",
    critical_fires_7d>100, "HIGH",
    days_since_update>365 OR mute_long==1, "MEDIUM",
    in_quiet_window==1 OR (stale_no_fire==0 AND critical_fires_7d<=100 AND days_since_update<=365), "LOW",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    health_class=="CRITICAL", "page_owner_review_signalflow_metric_names_and_prod_tags",
    health_class=="HIGH", "tune_noisy_thresholds_split_detectors_or_add_filters",
    health_class=="MEDIUM", "audit_mute_justification_refresh_detector_program",
    true(), "keep_governance_dashboard_only")
| table detector_id detector_name creator_id owner_team service health_class critical_fires_7d days_since_update days_since_trigger trigger_roll30 org_median_trig30 alerts_dm_hits lanes_seen recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_health_alert]
cron_schedule = 0 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_inventory_health | where health_class IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize hourly rollups per detector_id into a summary index and widen operational alerts to earliest=-7d while retaining this full search for investigations.

#### Understanding this SPL without decorative markup: the first bracketed arm scopes signalfx:detectors snapshots and normalizes identifiers. The second arm scopes signalfx:incidents, marks critical fires inside seven days, and pulses inc_pulse for rolling counts. The third arm scopes signalfx:audit_log for detector lifecycle verbs and mute hints. The fourth arm uses tstats against Alerts for signature keyed counts. The fifth arm scopes Terraform audit exports. The coalesce block repairs blank identifiers. streamstats time_window approximates thirty days of incident pulses per detector. stats aggregates last fire times and sums. join pulls governance columns from the CSV inventory. eventstats medians support peer context. case assigns health_class and recommended_action. The terminal table lists thirteen analyst-visible columns.

### Step 4 — Validate

API mute drill: create a known mute on a non-production detector through the Observability REST API, wait for the next signalfx:audit_log and signalfx:detectors snapshot, run observability_uc_13_5_12_detector_inventory_health, and confirm the row elevates toward MEDIUM or surfaces mute_long style signals with non-null recommended_action. Revoke the mute, wait for a clean snapshot, and confirm the row returns toward LOW for that detector when firing history is healthy.

Stale drill: create a test detector whose SignalFlow condition references an impossible threshold or a retired metric name, allow evaluation windows to elapse without incidents, and confirm stale_no_fire style logic marks governance concern for tier-one rows when the inventory lookup lacks infrastructure-as-code relief. Delete the test detector after capture.

Cross-source drill: pick a detector that fires weekly in Observability, confirm last_incident_ts in Splunk matches the UI incident timeline within one collection interval, and confirm critical_fires_7d matches a manual count from the incident feed for the same window.

Alerts datamodel drill: when Enterprise Security acceleration is healthy, confirm alerts_dm_hits moves for signatures that correspond to mirrored incidents. When acceleration is offline, document the blank arm and repair summaries before relying on correlation.

Terraform drill: apply a harmless metadata-only change through Terraform to a detector resource in sandbox, confirm terraform_cloud:audit rows land with matching resource identifiers, and confirm lanes_seen includes tf_audit for joined detectors when inventory maps addresses.

Negative test: on a tier-two service with healthy recent fires, quiet audit, fresh metadata, and modest seven-day counts, confirm health_class stays LOW and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to CRITICAL and HIGH only.

Runbook linkage: attach Observability detector editor deep links, SignalFlow program documentation anchors, team routing pages, and the quarterly governance slide deck location.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Hold a weekly thirty-minute governance review with the on-call rotation owner present, walk the CRITICAL and HIGH rows, assign JIRA remediation tickets per ABANDONED or STALE tier-one detector, publish an automated quarterly hygiene CSV from the same saved search, and refresh the executive dashboard tile that shows percent of detectors with fresh incidents and current programs.

Case A — OAuth access token expired on the scripted input: scripted pulls return 401, snapshots stop updating, and last_det_refresh ages; rotate credentials in the vault, replay a full pagination export, and backfill missing intervals before trusting mute logic.

Case B — Paginated detector list truncated after the first thousand objects: verify loop follows Link headers or offset cursors until completion; compare Splunk distinct detector_id count to Observability UI totals; fix pagination bugs before declaring coverage.

Case C — Audit log retention inside Observability shorter than thirty days while Splunk retention is longer: always mirror audits into Splunk early; if mirror jobs fail, mute governance may look cleaner than reality until you restore feeds.

Case D — Terraform-managed detectors flagged as ABANDONED because UI snapshots show no human edits: trust is_iac_managed and tf_audit lanes, suppress abandonment escalations when infrastructure-as-code flags are true, and teach reviewers that UI silence can be healthy for Terraform-owned objects.

Case E — Creator field is a service account causing mis-attribution: route ownership through owner_team in splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv and PagerDuty services keyed by team, not by API creator strings that always show automation identities.

Case F — service_criticality empty in the lookup for a tier-one production detector: defaulting to tier3 hides CRITICAL logic; treat empty criticality as a data defect, open a catalog ticket, and block promotion until the CSV row is complete.

Case G — scheduled_quiet_window vocabulary accidentally matches every hour: in_quiet_window may mask real staleness; constrain allowed tokens, validate CSV edits in peer review, and add unit tests for lookup freshness.

Case H — Mute set through Terraform never reflected in the UI snapshot sourcetype: compare Terraform state signals, raw API pulls, and Splunk sourcetype parsers; align field names for mute flags so snapshot_muted does not false-negative.

Case I — Detector disabled with enabled=false rather than is_muted: teach the search to treat disabled programs as intentionally quiet when exports include explicit disabled flags; extend parsers to map disabled to snapshot semantics so muting logic stays honest.

Case J — Seasonal commerce detectors flagged STALE during off-season: annotate scheduled_quiet_window with seasonal_campaign tags, adjust expected_trigger_freq_per_30d, and require human acknowledgment before auto-closing tickets in quiet quarters.

Case K — Duplicate detector_id values across two Observability orgs in a multi-tenant setup: add org_id or realm prefix to detector_id during ingest, include org_id in stats by clauses, and never join inventory rows without tenant keys.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, tstats, and comment appear; final table lists thirteen columns; Step 5 lists eleven lettered cases without decorative markup anywhere in this document.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.12 Observability Cloud detector governance: inventory snapshots, incident history, mute audit, Alerts datamodel facet, Terraform change audit. Tunables stale_trigger_days=90 mute_stale_days=7 earliest=-30d@d")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:detectors" earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(detectorId, id, detector_id, resourceId, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(name, detectorName, label, detector_name, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(creatorId, createdBy, author, creator_id, "")))
      | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(teamId, team, notification_team, "")))
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(service, service_name, sf_service, "")))
      | eval is_muted_now=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(isMuted, muted, is_muted, "false"))),"true|1"),1,0)
      | eval upd_epoch=if(isnotnull(lastUpdated), tonumber(lastUpdated,10), if(isnotnull('lastUpdated'), tonumber('lastUpdated',10), _time))
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="det_inv"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:incidents" earliest=-30d@d latest=now
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(detectorId, detector_id, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(detectorName, detector_name, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(incidentCreator, creator_id, "")))
      | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(responderTeam, teamId, team, "")))
      | eval service=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceId, service, "")))
      | eval sev_norm=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity, level, ""))))
      | eval is_crit_fire=if(_time>=relative_time(now(),"-7d@d") AND match(sev_norm,"critical|severe|error"),1,0)
      | eval is_muted_now=0
      | eval upd_epoch=_time
      | eval inc_pulse=1
      | eval lane="inc_hist"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:audit_log" earliest=-30d@d latest=now (action="detectorUpdated" OR action="detectorCreated" OR action="detectorMuted" OR match(lower(message),"detector|mute"))
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(targetId, objectId, detectorId, resourceId, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(targetName, detectorName, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(actorId, userEmail, "")))
      | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(teamScope, "")))
      | eval service=""
      | eval is_muted_now=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(action,message,""))),"mute|disable|silence"),1,0)
      | eval upd_epoch=_time
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="aud_mute"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count AS alert_dm_hit latest(_time) AS dm_last FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Alerts.signature
      | eval detector_id=md5(trim(toString(Alerts.signature)))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(Alerts.signature))
      | eval creator_id="splunk_cim_alerts"
      | eval team="es_correlation"
      | eval service="mapped_signature"
      | eval is_muted_now=0
      | eval upd_epoch=dm_last
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="alerts_dm"
      | eval _time=dm_last
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire alert_dm_hit ]
    [ search index=observability (sourcetype="terraform_cloud:audit" OR sourcetype="tfc:audit") earliest=-30d@d latest=now (resource_type="signalfx_detector" OR match(_raw,"signalfx_detector|detector"))
      | eval detector_id=trim(toString(coalesce(resource_id, resource_name, detectorId, "")))
      | eval detector_name=trim(toString(coalesce(resource_name, detector_name, "")))
      | eval creator_id=trim(toString(coalesce(user_name, actor, "")))
      | eval team="terraform_cloud"
      | eval service="iac_pipelined"
      | eval is_muted_now=0
      | eval upd_epoch=_time
      | eval inc_pulse=0
      | eval is_crit_fire=0
      | eval lane="tf_audit"
      | fields _time lane detector_id detector_name creator_id team service is_muted_now upd_epoch inc_pulse is_crit_fire ]
| eval detector_id=if(len(detector_id)<2 AND len(detector_name)>0, md5(detector_name), detector_id)
| eval detector_name=coalesce(nullif(detector_name,""), "unnamed_detector")
| eval creator_id=coalesce(nullif(creator_id,""), "unknown_creator")
| eval team=coalesce(nullif(team,""), "unassigned_team")
| eval service=coalesce(nullif(service,""), "unscoped_service")
| eval is_crit_fire=coalesce(is_crit_fire,0)
| sort 0 + detector_id + _time
| streamstats time_window=2592000 global=f sum(inc_pulse) AS triggers_30d BY detector_id
| stats
    max(eval(if(lane=="inc_hist",_time,null()))) AS last_incident_ts
    max(eval(if(lane=="det_inv",_time,null()))) AS last_det_refresh
    max(upd_epoch) AS last_metadata_epoch
    max(is_muted_now) AS snapshot_muted
    sum(is_crit_fire) AS critical_fires_7d
    sum(inc_pulse) AS incident_events_30d
    max(triggers_30d) AS trigger_roll30
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
    max(alert_dm_hit) AS alerts_dm_hits
  BY detector_id
| eval days_since_update=round((now()-last_metadata_epoch)/86400,2)
| join type=left max=0 detector_id
    [| inputlookup splunk_o11y_detector_inventory.csv
     | fields detector_id owner_team service_criticality is_iac_managed scheduled_quiet_window expected_trigger_freq_per_30d ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, team)
| eval service_criticality=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_criticality, "tier3"))))
| eval iac_flag=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce(is_iac_managed, "false"))),"true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval quiet_calendar=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(scheduled_quiet_window, ""))))
| eval in_quiet_window=if(match(quiet_calendar,"maint|blackout|holiday|training|sandbox"),1,0)
| eval days_since_trigger=if(isnull(last_incident_ts), 999, round((now()-last_incident_ts)/86400,2))
| eval stale_no_fire=if(isnull(last_incident_ts) OR days_since_trigger>90,1,0)
| eval mute_long=if(snapshot_muted==1 AND days_since_update>7,1,0)
| eval trig_metric=trigger_roll30
| eventstats median(trig_metric) AS org_median_trig30
| eval health_class=case(
    service_criticality=="tier1" AND stale_no_fire==1 AND iac_flag==0, "CRITICAL",
    critical_fires_7d>100, "HIGH",
    days_since_update>365 OR mute_long==1, "MEDIUM",
    in_quiet_window==1 OR (stale_no_fire==0 AND critical_fires_7d<=100 AND days_since_update<=365), "LOW",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    health_class=="CRITICAL", "page_owner_review_signalflow_metric_names_and_prod_tags",
    health_class=="HIGH", "tune_noisy_thresholds_split_detectors_or_add_filters",
    health_class=="MEDIUM", "audit_mute_justification_refresh_detector_program",
    true(), "keep_governance_dashboard_only")
| table detector_id detector_name creator_id owner_team service health_class critical_fires_7d days_since_update days_since_trigger trigger_roll30 org_median_trig30 alerts_dm_hits lanes_seen recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count AS alert_rows latest(_time) AS last_alert FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-30d@d latest=now BY Alerts.signature Alerts.severity
| rename Alerts.signature AS sig Alerts.severity AS sev
| table sig sev alert_rows last_alert
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by detector_id with badges for health_class, sparkline of trigger_roll30 versus org_median_trig30, drilldowns to raw signalfx:detectors JSON, signalfx:incidents timelines, signalfx:audit_log mute verbs, Terraform audit rows, and inventory lookup fields.

## Known False Positives

Approved maintenance windows legitimately mute detectors for hours or days; the scheduled_quiet_window column exists precisely so intentional silence does not auto-page platform leadership. Training and sandbox tenants often host deliberately quiet detectors used only during classes; exclude them with environment tags or separate indexes before treating silence as production risk. Seasonal retail detectors may fire only during peak commerce weeks; without expected_trigger_freq_per_30d hints, off-season STALE classifications look like defects even when the business expects quiet months. Creator-of-record frequently shows automation service accounts; never infer human abandonment from that field alone—pair with owner_team and Terraform audit actors. On-call rotations can make a healthy detector look ownerless when pagerduty_service strings lag a week behind HR changes; refresh roster lookups on the same cadence as people moves. Terraform-managed detectors intentionally show no UI edit history; treat infrastructure-as-code metadata as first-class evidence instead of flagging ABANDONED purely from last UI touch. Multi-environment scopes may reuse logical names across staging and production; missing org or realm keys can duplicate detector_id collisions that confuse joins—always partition by tenant. A detector that never fired may be stale, while a detector that fired once and was acknowledged can still be perfectly healthy; require incident outcome fields or manual notes before auto-closing tickets on first-fire patterns. Org-wide Observability lag metrics sometimes precede incident gaps; correlate stream lag signals before blaming individual SignalFlow programs. Enterprise Security Alerts acceleration gaps can zero out the tstats arm while raw incidents still flow; repair acceleration rather than muting the governance search. Lookup staleness makes service_criticality look empty; treat blank CSV rows as blocking defects for tier-one services. Quiet detectors bound to low-traffic canary services may be legitimately idle; use service tier metadata to avoid comparing them to checkout-scale expectations.
